# matomo_tagmanager_tag_livezilladynamic (Resource)

Livezilla lets you monitor and chat with visitors on your website.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "matomo" {}

resource "matomo_site" "example" {
  name = "Example LivezillaDynamic Site"
  urls = ["https://example-livezilladynamic.example.com"]
}

resource "matomo_tagmanager_container" "example" {
  site_id = matomo_site.example.id
  context = "web"
  name    = "Example LivezillaDynamic Container"
}

resource "matomo_tagmanager_trigger" "example" {
  container_id = matomo_tagmanager_container.example.id
  type         = "PageView"
  name         = "Example LivezillaDynamic Trigger"
}

resource "matomo_tagmanager_tag_livezilladynamic" "example" {
  container_id = matomo_tagmanager_container.example.id
  name         = "example-livezilladynamic"
  fire_trigger_ids = [matomo_tagmanager_trigger.example.id]
  livezilla_dynamic_id = "example-value"
  livezilla_dynamic_domain = "example-value"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `container_id` (String)
- `fire_trigger_ids` (List of String)
- `livezilla_dynamic_domain` (String) Enter the Domain where Livezilla is installed. Example: https://www.example.com
- `livezilla_dynamic_id` (String) Insert the Livezilla ID from your Dynamic Code section.
- `name` (String)

### Optional

- `block_trigger_ids` (List of String)
- `livezilla_dynamic_defer` (Boolean) In most cases you should let it activated.
- `status` (String)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
